rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application To Seal Documents In Support Of Oppos.'S To Pltf'S Mtns To Compel (Sets One, Three And Four)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD'S Application To Seal Documents In Support Of OpposITIONS To PlAINTIFF'S MOTIONS To Compel (Sets One, Three And Four). Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. As to all motions, including the motion to seal documents, counsel are directed to further meet and confer prior to the hearing to determine whether the discovery matters in dispute can be substantially limited. Counsel should be prepared, at the hearing, to fully explain the status of all four discovery matters. The case will be heard at the end of the discovery calendar, to facilitate full discussion between counsel (which should begin on Thursday). =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC, VS. KILLARNEY CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden. Defendant fails to establish that plaintiff is seeking only economic loss damages or that defendant's conduct did not cause any property damage. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC, VS. KILLARNEY CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CARDINAL CONSULTING INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this action against defendant Cardinal because UCB assigned its claim against Cardinal to plaintiff under the loan purchase agreement. See Cassidy Ex. 1 (Loan Purchase Agreement, §3). "Loan assets," as defined in the broadly deined to include causes of action "arising from or related to such Loans." (Id. §1.) The Court further finds that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Cardinal breached the UCB agreement and/or acted negligently. See, e.g., Reeves Declaration 5, 7 (as to 7A and 7B); 8, 14, 15, 17, 18, 24, 26, 27, 28. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2012, Line 21, Plaintiff Suntech America, Inc.'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Continued to August 31, 2012 per agreement of the parties. Moving party shall provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date, no later than August 25, 2012 at 4 pm. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITONE ,LLC VS. JOHN H WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT JOHN WHITE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued until September 14, 2012. Moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party shall provide courtesy copies to the Court by August 29, 2012. Moving party shall re-notice the hearing on the demurrer and serve it on Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
